heading: Transportation Systems Center working capital fund
label: 328 Transportation Systems Center working capital fund
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)328(a)49:1657(r)(1) (1st sentence, 2d sentence words before last comma, last sentence)., , , § 9(r); added , , .Oct. 15, 1966May 30, 1980Pub. L. 96–254, § 20794 Stat. 413328(b)49:1657(r)(1) (2d sentence words after last comma), (2)(B) (words after last comma).328(c)49:1657(r)(2)(A), (B) (words before last comma), (C).328(d)49:1657(r)(3).328(e)49:1657(r)(4).In subsection (a), the words “Department of Transportation has” are substituted for “Secretary is authorized to establish” because the working capital fund has been established. The words “The Transportation Systems Center is authorized to perform” are omitted as unnecessary because of the restatement. The words “in the fund” are added for clarity.

children.label: a
children.text: The Department of Transportation has a Transportation Systems Center working capital fund. Amounts in the fund are available for financing the activities of the Center, including research, development, testing, evaluation, analysis, and related activities the Secretary of Transportation approves, for the Department, other agencies, State and local governments, other public authorities, private organizations, and foreign countries.

children.label: b
children.text: Amounts in the fund are available without regard to fiscal year limitation. Amounts may be appropriated to the fund.
net assets of the Center as of , including unexpended advances made to the Center for which valid obligations were incurred before ;October 1, 1980October 1, 1980
the reasonable value of property and other assets transferred to the fund after , less the related liabilities and unpaid obligations; andSeptember 30, 1980
the reasonable value of property and other assets donated to the fund.

children.label: A
children.text: services at rates that will recover the expenses of operation, including the accrual of annual leave and overhead; and

children.label: B
children.text: acquiring property and equipment under regulations the Secretary prescribes; and
receipts from the sale or exchange of property or in payment for loss or damage of property held by the fund.

children.label: e
children.text: The Secretary shall deposit at the end of each fiscal year, in the Treasury as miscellaneous receipts, amounts accruing in the fund that the Secretary decides are in excess of the needs of the fund.
